what_it_does: A companion research/findings doc whose allocated area is whether the cognition swarm engine is real and reusable for whole-repo semantic reading. It reads the live cognition-swarm code, the resource manager, the service registry and a vLLM benchmark sheet, measures the actual repo size, and corrects the parent anchor's headline capacity/throughput numbers on three axes while affirming the engine exists and is mostly reusable.
observations: Marks every claim with an explicit evidence class: Observed (file:line/measured), Inferred, External, My-idea.

Catalogs concrete cognition.py functions as already-built: run_swarm (523-621), run_role (93-119), run_jury (637-715), resolve_run_ref (474-496), SlotBudget.from_registry (403-450), RoleRegistry.discover.
Corrects throughput from the anchor's ~3000 tok/s to a measured 2,241 tok/s aggregate at concurrency 32 (BENCHMARK_FACTSHEET.md:24).
Computes that live config caps max_num_seqs at 16, giving a swarm budget knee of 14 for small inputs and 8-13 for real source files (KV-bound).
Measures the real repo: ~395 first-party source files, ~1.37M tokens; core ~101 Python files; file-size distribution skewed into the 2-8K-token KV-binding band.
Flags runtime/suite.py at ~180K tokens as exceeding the 65,536 context window, requiring chunk-and-compose.
States VRAM is a non-issue (the 4B is already resident, loads nothing); the real contention is shared request-slot/KV between a sweep and the cognition stream's own waves.
standouts: Derives a per_role_ctx crossover at ~4.7K tokens/file where KV binding replaces the seq-cap, making effective concurrency file-size-dependent.
Identifies the one net-new code seam: run_role hardcodes content as 'Utterance: {utterance}' so a file-reading role needs a declared ctx-to-messages mapping.
Proposes giving a background sweep its own SlotBudget with a larger reserve_r so it is polite-by-construction and never queues a live wave behind it.
conventions: Follows the repo's evidence-classification discipline (Observed/Inferred/Verified-style marking) throughout.
Uses a TL;DR correction table up front, then sectioned argument, then a builder-facing bottom line.
Cites file:line and measured numbers rather than asserting from inference, per the project's no-speculation norm.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the grounded reality-check on whether the swarm engine can be pointed at the repo — ~80% reusable, but size the sweep at ~8-14 concurrent, ~1-2 minutes for the core, with suite.py needing chunking.
questions: How is the chunk-and-compose tier for over-window files and cross-file checks actually built, given the file says it is net-new?
What is the real prefill aggregate rate at the live concurrency of ~14 (the throughput interpolation is labelled Inferred)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated area: it asks whether the capability is real and the engine reusable, and delivers a grounded yes-with-three-corrections answer.
apparent_themes: Build-on-not-beside (reuse the existing wave engine rather than build a new one)
Honest cost accounting over optimistic headline numbers
Evidence-grounded correction as a stronger result than confirmation
Resource-budget discipline (slots, KV, reserve) as a first-class concern
intention_signals: It is trying to give a builder accurate sizing so a semantic-reading sweep is provisioned correctly — affirming feasibility while puncturing three optimistic assumptions about throughput, concurrency, and per-file context cost.
novelty: The per-file KV-crossover computation and the framing of contention as sweep-vs-cognition-waves (not sweep-vs-chat) are distinctive grounded derivations.
contribution: Contributes the measured capacity/throughput/concurrency reality for the swarm engine and the precise verbatim-vs-net-new reuse seam, including the suite.py context-window wall and the polite-budget coexistence mechanism.
summary_for_embedding: A grounded findings doc measuring the cognition swarm engine's real throughput, concurrency, context-window limits, and reuse seam for whole-repo semantic reading, correcting the anchor's headline numbers on three axes.
intention_for_embedding: To verify whether the existing swarm engine is real and reusable for repo-reading and to give a builder honest sizing for a semantic sweep.
